2603.reason: The error line number for missing semicolon should be the line of the last non-terminal before the semicolon, but here it uses preRead(-1) which gets the line of the token before the current one, which may not be correct if the current token is not the semicolon. Additionally, the error detection logic is flawed because it checks for semicolon after potentially parsing an expression, but if the expression parsing consumed tokens incorrectly, the semicolon check may fail.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  The error line number for missing semicolon should be the line of the last non-terminal before the semicolon, but here it uses preRead(-1) which gets the line of the token before the current one, which may not be correct if the current token is not the semicolon. Additionally, the error detection logic is flawed because it checks for semicolon after potentially parsing an expression, but if the expression parsing consumed tokens incorrectly, the semicolon check may fail.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  The error line number for missing semicolon should be the line of the last non-terminal before the semicolon, but here it uses preRead(-1) which gets the previous token's line number, which may not be correct if the previous token is not the last non-terminal of the statement.

3193.reason: IndexOutOfBoundsException: No boundary check is performed before accessing the list. If prePosition + position exceeds the list size, it will crash.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  IndexOutOfBoundsException: No boundary check is performed before accessing the list. If prePosition + position exceeds the list size, it will crash.

109.reason: [Error: preRead(2) may throw IndexOutOfBoundsException if fewer than 3 tokens remain, because hasNext() only guarantees at least 1 token exists.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  IndexOutOfBoundsException: hasNext() only guarantees at least one token exists, but preRead(2) attempts to access index position+2, which may be out of bounds.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: preRead(2) may throw IndexOutOfBoundsException if fewer than 3 tokens remain, because hasNext() only guarantees at least 1 token exists.]

121.reason: [Error: preRead(1) may throw IndexOutOfBoundsException if fewer than 2 tokens remain, because hasNext() only guarantees at least 1 token exists.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  IndexOutOfBoundsException: hasNext() guarantees at least one token, but preRead(1) accesses index position+1, which is out of bounds if only one token remains.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: preRead(1) may throw IndexOutOfBoundsException if fewer than 2 tokens remain, because hasNext() only guarantees at least 1 token exists.]

2862.reason: StringIndexOutOfBoundsException: positionNum is incremented without checking bounds before accessing the character.

3050.reason: StringIndexOutOfBoundsException: positionNum might equal inputLine.length() if the identifier is at the end of the file, causing an out-of-bounds access.

338.reason: [The code unconditionally consumes the next token assuming it's '=', but doesn't verify if the current token is actually ASSIGN. This will cause incorrect parsing or errors if the token isn't '='.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The code unconditionally consumes the next token assuming it's '=', but doesn't verify if the current token is actually ASSIGN. This will cause incorrect parsing or errors if the token isn't '='.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This line unconditionally consumes the next token without checking if it's actually an ASSIGN token. According to the grammar ConstDef → Ident [ '[' ConstExp ']' ] '=' ConstInitVal, there must be an '=' token here. If it's missing, this code will consume whatever token comes next, leading to incorrect parsing and potentially masking the real error. It should check if the current token is ASSIGN before calling next(), and report an error if it's missing.]
